psyche_asks_all.note: Prompt narrative cited 1559; file 'total' field and len(asks) both report 310. Numbers below are against the 310 available asks.

fields_legend.axis_gap_filled: one of: criterion_referenceability | retrieval_vs_reconstruction | handle_density | target_slice | under_specification
fields_legend.primitives: list of the primitive signals that could score the ask (time window, artifact path, commit, count, etc.)
fields_legend.paraphrase_of: probe id if paraphrase, else null

candidates.ask: where were we? what was the last session working on?
candidates.notes: Near-verbatim R08 paraphrase; useful for paraphrase robustness.

candidates.ask: what's going on?
candidates.notes: Extreme zero-handle case; tests whether agent bootstraps a tip frame with no scaffolding.

candidates.ask: what am I working on?
what am I working on right now in syke?
candidates.notes: Minimal-handle variant of R01.

candidates.ask: what should I be working on right now?
candidates.notes: Prescriptive orientation; not present in 19-probe set. Forces tip-state with a next-step demand but zero handles.

candidates.ask: what are my active threads right now?
candidates.notes: Low-handle R09 paraphrase; extends the THREAD_MAP demand into zero-scaffold form.

candidates.ask: what was my last claude-code session about?
candidates.notes: Harness-scoped variant of R14 (opencode). Adds per-harness tip resolution coverage.

candidates.ask: what was the last thing recorded about the april 6-7 session? what open loops were flagged?
candidates.notes: Pairs date-bounded verifiable surface with semantic 'flagged loops' judgement.

candidates.ask: what happened on March 21 2026? what runs did we do, how many synthesis cycles, what was the activity that caused 72 million input tokens?
candidates.notes: Pure QUANT+TIME_WINDOW; every primitive is countable. Surfaces enumeration_completeness blind spot identified in ASK_DEMAND_TAXONOMY.

candidates.ask: What do the logs show about actual syke synthesis usage? Token counts, costs, duration, how many turns per cycle, prompt sizes — any metrics from real synthesis and ask runs.
candidates.notes: Log-grounded QUANT ask. 19-probe set has QUANT (R02, R04, R05, R17) but none against live metric logs.

candidates.ask: Give me the full list of all architectural loops in syke — observe, adapter, synthesis, ask, healing, self-scaffolding, experiments, all of them. For each one, tell me: is it closed (working), open (not done), or broken. Check the events DB for recent activity, check the memex, check everything you have.
candidates.notes: High-handle R05 cousin with an explicit per-item status classification demand.

candidates.ask: Read the adapter markdown at ~/.syke/data/saxenauts/adapters/claude-code/adapter.md. Then check what claude-code data exists at the paths it describes. How many sessions do I have?
candidates.notes: DOC_SYNTH + COMMITTED_STATE: read a named file and reconcile with filesystem truth. Currently 0 DOC_SYNTH coverage in R01-R19.

candidates.ask: what's the current state of the repo and research? what are my latest instructions and open loops? what branch am I on and what's dirty?
candidates.notes: Adds committed-truth fidelity (branch, dirty) to the R01 shape. Introduces CONFIG_CHECK-adjacent primitives.

candidates.ask: what is the current state of pi_ask.py rework and what is the uncommitted work trying to solve? Also confirm: is the 19-item Azure-mini benchmark smoke still running, did the race fix hold, and what is the latest on MEMEX projection leak?
candidates.notes: Combines COMMITTED_STATE (uncommitted diff, running process) with DECISION_RECALL. No analogue in 19-probe set.

candidates.ask: Codex reports the 15-day zero replay is over. Confirm: did the 15-day zero replay complete? Full cycle count, final artifact location, any errors or interrupts, and what is the current state of the 19-item benchmark smoke? Also give the latest MEMEX projection leak counts as of right now.
candidates.notes: Confirms-a-report shape: user asserts claim, agent verifies. New task family (FACT_CHECK_CLAIM).

candidates.ask: What is the latest OMX feedback or conversation? Any recent omx sessions, artifacts, or plans?
candidates.notes: Fresh-tip ask scoped to a named surface; narrow but under-represented angle on R14.

candidates.ask: What new docs or changes did codex create in the last hour or two? Check _internal/analysis/ and any other new files. What is the latest eval design thinking?
candidates.notes: Sub-day window + directory-scoped file check — harder temporal resolution than any current probe.

candidates.ask: list every open loop, unfinished task, known bug, next step, and unresolved question across all routes — be exhaustive
candidates.notes: Zero-handle exhaustive demand; tests enumeration completeness without any seed list (R05 provides seeds).

candidates.ask: What have we been able to do with the NE-1 / N=1 benchmark thesis so far, what have we created with bundles, and what is left to finish? Be specific about delivered artifacts, evidence, and remaining gaps.
candidates.notes: GAP_ANALYSIS — none present in R01-R19.

candidates.ask: On February 23, was there a case where current state contradicted what had been established earlier?
candidates.notes: CONTRADICTION-DETECTION on a past date. Operative-state-vs-prior-frame check; no analogue in 19-probe.

candidates.ask: Look at my work over the past month across all harnesses (use adapters at ~/.syke/adapters/). Are there decisions I made and then reversed? Threads I started and abandoned? Recurring themes I keep coming back to? What patterns do you see that I might not be aware of?
candidates.notes: PATTERN_DISCOVERY (meta-self-observation). No 19-probe analogue; pure reconstruction-over-cross-surface.

candidates.ask: Read MEMEX.md in the workspace. What are my active projects and what am I working on?
candidates.notes: DOC_SYNTH variant of R01; judge can score file-reading compliance + synthesis against slice.

candidates.ask: Read MEMEX.md. What are my top 3 active projects right now? Be brief.
candidates.notes: Constrained variant: k=3 answer and 'be brief'. Tests format adherence as judge dimension (new).

candidates.ask: what is the history of hacks and patches in this codebase? specifically around the LiteLLM proxy, Kimi middleware, and any known technical debt or workarounds we've been accumulating
candidates.notes: Deeper CHANGE_HISTORY than R13 (adapter-as-compiler) — codebase-scale patch archaeology.

candidates.ask: what did opencode change in litellm_proxy.py today? specifically the patches for thinking, _azure_translate_patched, _azure_process_event_patched flags, and the reordering of patches before litellm imports. check the opencode events from the last 3 hours
candidates.notes: Three-hour window + named-symbol diff. Pure hard-verifiable; fills the precision-window gap.

candidates.ask: Did I decide to use Docker for Syke's sandboxing or not? Check all harnesses using adapters at ~/.syke/adapters/. I want to know if there was a decision and whether it changed over time.
candidates.notes: DECISION + CHANGE_OVER_TIME + CROSS_SOURCE — three demands at once. Current judge schema has no DECISION home.

candidates.ask: what are the user's exact latest instructions and perspective they just gave in codex? I want their full words, directives, and framing — not a summary. What did they literally say about how to approach the research, what matters, what to do next? Include their exact quotes and instructions verbatim where possible.
candidates.notes: VERBATIM_QUOTATION demand. Flips normal paraphrase-tolerance expectation. META_HANDOFF adjacent.

candidates.ask: Using adapters at ~/.syke/adapters/, trace the 'sandbox design' thread across ALL harnesses. I want every session that touched this topic, in every tool, with timestamps. Show me the chain — how did this idea move between tools? What connected each step to the next? What was the route?
candidates.notes: PROVENANCE-CHAIN demand (route between sessions). R07 asks for timeline; this asks for causal chain. Distinct.

candidates.ask: What was I doing on April 4th, 2026? Check all harnesses using the adapters at ~/.syke/adapters/. Reconstruct the day — what happened, in what order, across which tools.
candidates.notes: Cross-surface variant of R04 (single-harness day log). Hardens CROSS_SOURCE on a bounded day.

candidates.ask: Is there a connection between the February 23 forgotten-plan episode and the March 13-14 cross-harness work?
candidates.notes: LONG_RANGE_CAUSAL_LINK across distant dates. No 19-probe analogue. Tests reconstruction across non-adjacent windows.

candidates.ask: Debug the current mismatch: why do some NE-1.3 evals expect cross-harness evidence at dates where the slice is Claude-only? I want the real history: how the bundle/data was defined, how the probes were created, what changed in the dataset story, and whether this is a bug in slicing or stale probe design. Be precise.
candidates.notes: META / SELF-REFERENTIAL on the benchmark itself. Reconstruction axis with a judgement demand.

candidates.ask: What papers and research have we studied on LLM-as-judge design, eval scoring, RSI evaluation, and benchmark judge mechanisms? Include any arxiv papers, blog posts, or research notes. Also any references to Deep Agents, HyperAgents, JADE, EvalPlanner, or similar eval frameworks.
candidates.notes: High-handle CONCEPT_RECALL with named-entity grounding. R10 is the closest existing analogue.

candidates.ask: we compiled a huge list of memory systems and competitors, probably had dozens of entries. find that full list from recent sessions. it was for the omocon talk landscape. check the last few days of sessions
candidates.notes: ARTIFACT_RECONSTRUCTION across recent sessions. Tests whether the agent can find a compiled artifact vs re-compile.
